chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

questions.question: Explain the main causes of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: The Revolt of 1857 was caused by political, economic and military reasons. Indian rulers lost their kingdoms, peasants suffered from heavy taxes and soldiers were unhappy with discrimination and the greased cartridge issue.

questions.question: Describe how the Revolt of 1857 began at Meerut.
questions.answer: In May 1857, sepoys at Meerut refused to use the new cartridges. When they were punished, soldiers rebelled, killed British officers and marched to Delhi.

questions.question: Explain the role of Bahadur Shah Zafar in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Bahadur Shah Zafar was declared the leader of the rebels in Delhi. His leadership gave legitimacy to the revolt.

questions.question: Describe the contribution of Rani Lakshmibai in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi fought bravely against British forces and became a symbol of resistance during the revolt.

questions.question: Explain the role of Nana Sahib in the revolt.
questions.answer: Nana Sahib led the revolt in Kanpur and opposed British rule after being denied his pension by the British.

questions.question: Describe the spread of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: The revolt spread from Meerut to Delhi, Kanpur, Lucknow, Jhansi and other parts of northern and central India.

questions.question: Explain the role of sepoys in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Sepoys started the revolt by rebelling against British officers due to discrimination and religious concerns.

questions.question: Why did the greased cartridge issue create anger among soldiers?
questions.answer: The cartridges were believed to be greased with cow and pig fat, which offended the religious beliefs of Hindu and Muslim soldiers.

questions.question: Explain how the British suppressed the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: The British used strong military force, modern weapons and loyal soldiers to recapture cities and suppress the rebellion.

questions.question: Explain the consequences of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: After the revolt, the British Crown took direct control of India and ended the rule of the East India Company.

questions.question: Describe the changes in administration after the revolt.
questions.answer: After the revolt, the British government ruled India directly and appointed a Viceroy to govern the country.

questions.question: Explain why the British reorganised the army after the revolt.
questions.answer: The British reorganised the army to prevent future rebellions and increased the number of European soldiers.

questions.question: Explain how Indian rulers participated in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Several Indian rulers joined the revolt to oppose British expansion and protect their kingdoms.

questions.question: Explain the role of peasants in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Many peasants supported the revolt because they were unhappy with high taxes and British policies.

questions.question: Explain the importance of Delhi during the revolt.
questions.answer: Delhi became the centre of the revolt after rebels declared Bahadur Shah Zafar as their leader.

questions.question: Explain why the revolt spread rapidly in northern India.
questions.answer: Many groups including soldiers, rulers and peasants were unhappy with British rule and joined the rebellion.

questions.question: Explain the impact of the revolt on British policies.
questions.answer: The revolt forced the British government to change its policies and rule India more cautiously.

questions.question: Explain why the Revolt of 1857 is considered an important event in Indian history.
questions.answer: It was the first large-scale uprising against British rule and showed the growing resistance among Indians.

questions.question: Explain the role of Kanpur during the revolt.
questions.answer: Kanpur was an important centre of the revolt led by Nana Sahib.

questions.question: Explain the role of Lucknow during the revolt.
questions.answer: Lucknow became a major centre of resistance where rebels fought British forces for control of the city.

questions.question: Explain the role of Jhansi during the revolt.
questions.answer: Jhansi became an important centre of resistance led by Rani Lakshmibai.

questions.question: Explain why the revolt lacked unity among rebels.
questions.answer: Different groups had different goals and there was no single leader to coordinate the rebellion.

questions.question: Explain the role of leadership in the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: Leaders such as Rani Lakshmibai, Nana Sahib and Bahadur Shah Zafar inspired people to fight against British rule.

questions.question: Explain the reasons for the failure of the Revolt of 1857.
questions.answer: The revolt failed due to lack of unity, weak organisation and the military strength of the British.

questions.question: Explain how the Revolt of 1857 changed the governance of India.
questions.answer: After the revolt, the British government abolished the East India Company and took direct control of India.
